ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for alert suppression: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Support read, validate, mutate, rollback, and audit workflows for alert suppression with disaster-recovery guardrails.
backend_scope: Add validated commands, dry-run support, RBAC checks, and audit records for alert suppression.
frontend_scope: Add action menu, confirmations, diff previews, and result status for alert suppression.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Alerting, Notification, and On-Call alert suppression operations with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: alert-suppression_disaster-recovery_operations evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for alert suppression disaster-recovery that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for alert suppression; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for alert suppression.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for alert suppression recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for alert suppression.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for alert suppression.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing alert suppression data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling alert suppression operations broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for alert suppression.
acceptance_criteria: Given alert suppression data exists, when the disaster-recovery operations workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: alert suppression connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for maintenance window: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Let an agent investigate maintenance window scalability issues with bounded tool use, read-only diagnostics first, explicit uncertainty, and approval-gated mutations.
backend_scope: Add tool registry, planner, execution trace, read-only default policy, token/time budgets, rollback plan schema, and stop conditions for maintenance window.
frontend_scope: Add investigation timeline, tool-call evidence, confidence labels, approval prompts, replay, and promote-to-runbook actions for maintenance window.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Alerting, Notification, and On-Call maintenance window agentic-investigation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: maintenance-window_scalability_agentic-investigation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for maintenance window scalability that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for maintenance window; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for maintenance window.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for maintenance window recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for maintenance window.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for maintenance window.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing maintenance window data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling maintenance window agentic-investigation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for maintenance window.
acceptance_criteria: Given maintenance window data exists, when the scalability agentic-investigation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: maintenance window connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for Teams notification: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover Teams notification, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the scalability pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for Teams notification.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for Teams notification.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Alerting, Notification, and On-Call Teams notification inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: teams-notification_scalability_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for Teams notification scalability that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for Teams notification; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for Teams notification.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for Teams notification recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for Teams notification.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for Teams notification.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing Teams notification data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling Teams notification inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for Teams notification.
acceptance_criteria: Given Teams notification data exists, when the scalability inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: Teams notification connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for webhook delivery.
acceptance_criteria: Given webhook delivery data exists, when the security health workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
